mode: normal (continuation — final 2 modules of the course)
scope_note: Prior run (2026-05-15T16-07-06Z) executed the M7-M12 plan but only completed M7/M8/M9/M10 end-to-end before context exhaustion. This run picks up the final two modules (M11 + M12) and closes the course. With M11 and M12 done, the entire 12-module AP Calculus AB curriculum has slides + script.json + reviews on disk, awaiting Mac launchd for TTS + merge + upload.
throughput_strategy: Spawned 2 end-to-end module producer sub-agents in parallel (one per remaining module). Each ran its own internal generator → 3-reviewer cascade → optional round-2 revise → lesson writer → build_slides verify chain. Aggregated reports below.

course_completion_status.ap_calculus_ab: COMPLETE on the auto-pipeline side. All 12 modules have script.json + slides/*.png + music. Awaiting Mac launchd for: TTS audio synthesis, ffmpeg slide-concat + audio-merge into .mp4, YouTube upload (capped at 4/day).

next_run_targets: AP Calculus AB course is done. Next scheduled run should pivot to the next-priority course per AUTO_PIPELINE Step 1 selection rules.

Candidates (in priority order suggested for next run): (a) verify AP Computer Science A modules 1-10 produced earlier are complete/uploaded, (b) start a new AP course that has a CED reference (consider: AP Biology if reference is added, AP Statistics, AP US History). The selector should check `references/` for the next *-ced.md doc before picking.
anomalies: Both sub-agents reported zero collision when working in parallel on adjacent module folders. Neither tried to write outside its assigned slug.

Working tree had pre-existing modifications on .gitignore, tools/lesson-video/*.py, tools/youtube-upload/*.py, tools/lesson-video/SKILL.md (carryover from Alan's Mac WIP). Per prior-run convention these are NOT included in this commit — only newly-produced lesson assets are staged.
